activation_results.reason: Input is a verbatim match of the skill's described purpose.
Same scope with Audit swapped for Review; clearly within the skill's remit.
User explicitly invokes the skill by name.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to IaC auditing.
Generic question about skills is not an IaC audit request.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative uses neutral/AU spellings (e.g., 'Audited', 'Generated by ... Anthril') with no American-only spellings like 'color' or 'behavior'.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register cites e.g. 'modules/vpc/main.tf:42', 'environments/prod/backend.tf:1', and severities CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM are used throughout.
Artefact contains Executive Summary, Module Dependency Graph, Category Scores, Per-Module Findings, Live Plan Output, Risk Register, Action Batches, Suppressed.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)

judge_result.notes: Criterion 3 lists no explicit sections (trailing colon), so it is treated as satisfied by the presence of the canonical sections shown in the example. All other criteria are clearly met: AU/neutral English, file:line evidence with severity bands, and concrete named targets rather than placeholders. Verdict set to 'partial' only because criterion 3's section list was empty in the prompt; functionally the artefact looks pass-grade.
